heroSummary: A structured five-level model for understanding and developing proficiency with Claude Code as an AI-assisted development tool.
purpose: This framework defines five levels of competence for working with Claude Code, ranging from basic tool operation to designing agentic systems.
It provides a common vocabulary for discussing AI coding proficiency and a structured path for skill development.
whatThisIs: This is a maturity model — a structured ladder that describes observable behaviors, core skills, and knowledge at each stage of Claude Code proficiency.
It is not a certification or a test. It is a reference framework for self-assessment and guided learning.
howToUseThisFramework: Start by reading the level summaries to find where you currently sit. Then explore the dimensions to understand what cross-cutting skills matter at every level.
Use the matrix to see how each dimension evolves across levels. Use the level detail pages to understand graduation criteria and evaluation tasks.

levelSummaries.title: Operator
levelSummaries.summary: Uses Claude Code as a command-line tool with basic prompting.

levelSummaries.title: Structured Collaborator
levelSummaries.summary: Structures interactions with context, constraints, and verification.

levelSummaries.title: Environment Builder
levelSummaries.summary: Configures persistent environments with CLAUDE.md, hooks, and MCP.

levelSummaries.title: Workflow Engineer
levelSummaries.summary: Designs multi-agent workflows and CI/CD integration.

levelSummaries.title: Agentic Systems Expert
levelSummaries.summary: Architects governance, safety, and organizational AI coding strategy.
